verdict: Moderate risk of AI displacement, with some tasks being highly automatable but others requiring human creativity and judgment.

reasoning_steps.title: High routine cognitive tasks
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks like singing a cappella or with musical accompaniment, and sight-reading musical parts during rehearsals are highly automatable by current LLMs.

reasoning_steps.title: Creative judgment required
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks like interpreting or modifying music, applying knowledge of harmony, melody, rhythm, and voice production to individualize presentations and maintain audience interest require human creativity and judgment.

adjacent_careers.name: Singer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both musicians and singers perform music, but singers may not have the same level of instrumental expertise.

adjacent_careers.name: Instrumentalist
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Instrumentalists play musical instruments, which can be highly automatable by current LLMs.

recent_developments.headline: LLMs improve music generation capabilities
recent_developments.impact: This development may make some music-related tasks more automatable in the future.

recent_developments.headline: Generative tools enter mainstream creative pipelines
recent_developments.impact: Studios and agencies use AI for ideation, layout, voice, and music drafts — finals still human-finished.

quotes.quote: I am utterly disgusted. This is an insult to life itself.
quotes.speaker: Hayao Miyazaki
quotes.title: Co-founder, Studio Ghibli

quotes.quote: AI is a tool. It will not replace artists. It will replace artists who do not learn to use AI as a tool.
quotes.speaker: Refik Anadol
quotes.title: Media artist

quotes.quote: Generative tools collapse the cost of producing a draft to near zero. The differentiator becomes taste, judgment, and knowing what is worth making at all.
quotes.speaker: John Maeda
quotes.title: Designer; VP of Design and AI, Microsoft
